title: Commercial Strip Mall FDC / Standpipe Supply

hoses: Supply line from hydrant to Engine 181 intake
FDC Supply Line A: 100 ft of 2½-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to building FDC
FDC Supply Line B: 100 ft of 2½-inch hose from Engine 181 discharge to building FDC
Interior attack line: 150 ft of 2½-inch hose from standpipe outlet to nozzle
appliances: Building FDC / standpipe system, 25 PSI system loss
sceneElements.elevation: Grade-level commercial occupancy, 0 PSI elevation correction

problems.title: Known Standpipe Flow With Unknown Sprinkler Flow
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying one interior standpipe attack line using a 1⅛-inch smooth bore nozzle at 50 PSI. A separate sprinkler FDC support line is connected, but the sprinkler system flowing GPM is unknown. Using smooth-bore flow = 29.7 × diameter² × √NP, and not adding unknown sprinkler flow as a known demand, what known standpipe attack flow should be used for the operation?
formulaBreakdown: Flow ≈ 265 GPM for the standpipe attack line.
Sprinkler FDC flow is unknown, so do not add an assumed sprinkler GPM.
problems.instructorExplanation: This scenario has a known standpipe attack flow and an unknown sprinkler support flow. Calculate the known standpipe flow from the smooth-bore nozzle, but do not invent or add a sprinkler GPM value.
problems.explainMistake: Do not add a guessed sprinkler support flow. Unless the scenario gives a measured sprinkler flow, sprinkler FDC support should be treated as unknown flow and handled as a pressure-support operation.

problems.title: FDC Supply Line Friction Loss
problems.question: The building FDC is supplied by two equal 100 ft 2½-inch lines. The known standpipe attack flow is 265 GPM, while the separate sprinkler support flow is unknown and excluded. Split the known standpipe flow equally between the two FDC supply lines. Using FL = C × (GPM/100)² × (length/100), with C = 2, what is the friction loss in each FDC supply line?
formulaBreakdown: The FDC is supplied by two equal supply lines.
Flow per FDC supply line = 265 ÷ 2 = 132.5 GPM.
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
FDC supply line length = 100 ft = 1 hundred.
problems.instructorExplanation: Because the two FDC supply lines are equal, the total flow is split evenly between them. Each line carries 132.5 GPM, producing approximately 5 PSI friction loss.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate each FDC supply line using the full 265 GPM. The two equal lines split the flow before entering the FDC.

problems.title: Sixth-Floor Standpipe Elevation
problems.question: An interior standpipe attack is operating on the 6th floor. Use the elevation formula: height = (floor number - 1) × 10 ft, and elevation pressure = height × 0.5 PSI/ft. What elevation pressure must be added?
formulaBreakdown: 6th floor is 5 floors above the 1st floor.
problems.instructorExplanation: Elevation is based on floors above the first floor, not the floor number itself.
problems.explainMistake: Do not multiply the 6th floor directly by 10 ft. Use the formula height = (floor number - 1) × 10 ft because the 1st floor is grade level.

problems.title: Total Pump Discharge Pressure
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a grade-level standpipe attack through two equal 100 ft 2½-inch FDC supply lines. The known attack flow is 265 GPM; split that flow equally between the two FDC supply lines. The interior attack line is 150 ft of 2½-inch hose flowing 265 GPM through a 1⅛-inch smooth bore at 50 PSI. Add 25 PSI FDC/standpipe system loss and no elevation. What pump discharge pressure should be set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FL + NP + elevation + appliance loss.
FDC supply line friction loss = 5 PSI.
Interior attack line FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds.
Interior attack line uses 2½-inch hose, so C = 2.
Interior attack line length = 150 ft = 1.5 hundreds.
Round interior FL to nearest 5 PSI: 20 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: The pump must overcome the FDC supply hose friction loss, the interior attack line friction loss, nozzle pressure, and the FDC / standpipe system loss. The total required pump discharge pressure is 100 PSI.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate only the hose from the engine to the FDC. A complete standpipe supply calculation also includes the interior attack line, nozzle pressure, and standpipe system loss.

problems.title: Elevated Sprinkler FDC Support Pressure
problems.question: For a sprinkler-only FDC support operation, the sprinkler system flowing GPM is unknown. Use the department sprinkler/FDC guideline: PDP = 150 PSI base + elevation pressure. Elevation pressure = (floor number - 1) × 10 ft × 0.5 PSI/ft. The sprinkler area being supported is on the 4th floor. No flow-based friction loss or other losses are used. What pump discharge pressure should be set?
formulaBreakdown: Sprinkler flowing GPM is unknown, so no flow-based friction loss is calculated.
Base sprinkler FDC support setting = 150 PSI.
problems.instructorExplanation: Use the sprinkler-only support base setting, then add the stated elevation correction. Do not invent a sprinkler GPM value.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate this like an interior handline, and do not invent a sprinkler system flow. It is a sprinkler-support setting with the stated elevation correction.
